events.label: reasoning
events.text:
>
{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "in-progress", "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 50, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0, "rate": 0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Adding water on site to manipulate slump is prohibited unless authorized by the engineer and within specification limits; this practice weakens concrete, increases water-cement ratio, and can cause segregation, bleeding, and reduced durability.", "On-site water addition without documented approval violates ACI 301 and ACI 304 requirements for concrete mixture control and placement quality.", "Uncontrolled water addition creates a latent defect in the installed concrete that may not be detectable after placement, risking structural capacity and service life."], "actions": ["Stop the concrete placement immediately and quarantine the affected load/batch.", "Do not place any concrete load to which unauthorized water has been added.", "Notify the site superintendent, quality control technician, and engineer of record.", "Document the batch tickets, time, location, and quantity of water added; retain samples if possible.", "Follow the project quality plan for disposition: reject the load or obtain written engineer approval with adjusted mix design and testing.", "Re-brief the crew on the approved concrete handling and placement procedures before resuming work."], "rationale": "Adding water at the jobsite to increase slump is a common but prohibited practice because it increases the water-cement ratio beyond the approved mix design, reducing strength and durability and potentially causing segregation. ACI 301 and ACI 304 permit water addition only under strict, engineer-approved controls. Because the work is actively in progress and the concrete is being placed non-compliantly, the decision is fail and the risk is high due to latent structural and durability impacts.", "references": ["ACI 301 Specifications for Structural Concrete", "ACI 304 Guide for Measuring, Mixing, Transporting, and Placing Concrete"]}
